content.text: Businesses offer employees rewards in order to
prevent the possibility of industrial action
recognise the value of their effort to the business.
provide employees with an income to meet the costs of living.

Which of the following would be the main performance objective being targeted by this
florist?

stimulus.text:
A florist designs floral displays for weddings to suit the colour preferences of individual
couples.

content.text: Which of the following best illustrates $\text{relationship marketing}$?
Using promotions to increase customer sales
Basing the marketing mix on customer feedback
As a result of this injury, what support is this employee entitled to?

stimulus.text: An employee receives an electrical burn while working. The employee requires hospital treatment and is unable to return to work for three months

content.text: Which marketing strategy would most likely be used?

stimulus.text: A business wants to quickly achieve a large market share for a product.

content.text: Which influence on human resources is most likely to have led to this?

stimulus.text:
A mattress manufacturer reduced the working hours of its factory workers due to a
decrease in demand.

content.text: Which of the following shows the correct order of the steps in a marketing plan?

Market research, establishing market objectives, identifying target markets,
situational analysis

Establishing market objectives, identifying target markets, situational analysis,
market research

Identifying target markets, situational analysis, market research, establishing
market objectives

Situational analysis, market research, establishing market objectives, identifying
target markets
Which form of payment has the lowest level of risk for the exporter?

stimulus.text: An export company based in Sydney sells Australian-made art to the Asian market.

content.text:
The training of staff in the manufacturing process is an example of interdependence
between which of the following business functions?
Which marketing strategy is being used?

stimulus.text: An energy drink business uses a celebrity to promote its products online.

content.text:
Which of the following is involved in making workplace agreements and resolving
workplace disputes?
Which long-term source of finance would be most appropriate for this system?

stimulus.text:
An Australian theme park wants to implement a new point-of-sale system that allows
improved tracking of stock levels. The system will cost $185 000 to establish.

content.text:
Under consumer law, why must the business refund the customer the cost of the mobile
phone?

stimulus.text:
A customer returns a recently purchased 5G mobile phone to the place of purchase as the
phone was faulty.

content.text: It is ethical to replace defective goods.
It is within the implied conditions of the contract.
It is important to meet corporate social responsibility.
It can be considered deceptive and misleading advertising.

What impact would this have on the goods imported to Australia and the goods exported
to the United States?

Imports: More expensive
Exports: More expensive

Imports: Less expensive
Exports: More expensive

Imports: Less expensive
Exports: Less expensive

Imports: More expensive
Exports: Less expensive

stimulus.text: The Australian dollar increases from 0.50 to 0.70 USD

content.text: Which of the following best describes this operations management strategy?

stimulus.text:
A large construction company has decided to focus their operations on design and
innovation.

content.text: What is the net profit ratio for this business in 2022? (Net profit ratio = net profit ÷ sales)

stimulus.text: The table shows extracts from the financial statements of a business for 2022.D

content.text: Which tasks could be occurring at the same time?

stimulus.text: A critical path analysis is shown for a job.

content.text:
Task $\text{EC}$ is delayed and takes an additional 7 days.

By how many days will the length of the critical path change?

stimulus.text: A critical path analysis is shown for a job.

content.text:
Which of the following stakeholders has the power to implement workplace reforms to
support employees of various abilities, ages and genders?

Which strategy would be most effective in improving the current ratio for this business?
(Current ratio = current assets ÷ current liabilities)

stimulus.text: The following is an extract from a business balance sheet.

content.text: Borrow money on a short-term bank loan
Take on a new mortgage to purchase buildings
Offer a discount to customers for early payment

stimulus.text:
An Australian tyre manufacturer is expanding its business. In order to reduce operating costs and increase sales and profit, rubber will be sourced from the global market. 

The business wants to:
- maintain quality outputs
- achieve economies of scale.

content.text:
Outline ONE potential issue this business could face in relation to both cost and
quality of outputs.

Explain the benefits of achieving economies of scale for both the business and
consumers.
Explain why both staff and management might be resistant to this change.

stimulus.text:
The owners of the tyre manufacturing business have purchased leading-edge
technology to help achieve economies of scale.

content.text: What is the purpose of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) legislation?
Outline TWO human resource issues found in this advertisement.

stimulus.text: The advertisement for a store manager is shown.

content.text:
Explain the social influences that have contributed to the changing labour force
participation rates in the period 1981−2021.

stimulus.text:
The table shows labour force participation rates for males and females in
Australia, 1981−2021.

content.text: Outline the current product life cycle stage of this cereal.

stimulus.text: How could a *niche market approach* increase sales for this cereal manufacturer?

content.text:
Justify ONE global marketing strategy this cereal manufacturer could use to
expand into the global market.

stimulus.text: A cereal manufacturer has decided to change the packaging of its product in an attempt to reverse a drop in sales and remain competitive with existing businesses. It is also considering growing its target market by catering for health-conscious consumers in Australia and eventually expanding into the global market.

content.text: What is the purpose of a cash flow statement?

Explain a possible conflict between ONE short-term and ONE long-term
financial objective.
Explain TWO limitations of financial reports.

The directors have asked you to write a business report in which you:
- outline some of the challenges including the global factors of costs, skills and supply the
business may need to consider in recruiting and retaining staff
- compare the use of debt and equity as sources of finance
- recommend ONE source of finance this business could use to fund its expansion.

stimulus.text:
Future Century Gaming Pty Ltd operates an online gaming platform targeting players aged 18−40 in Australia. It has been in operation since 2012, providing subscription access to a variety of online games.

The business is considering expanding into emerging global markets but is finding it challenging to recruit and retain skilled, qualified game designers and developers in Australia. It is considering international labour markets to overcome staff shortages.

The business has decided to finance its expansion into global markets through
external sources.

content.text:
To what extent do influences on marketing affect business success? 

OR

To what extent do influences on operations affect business success?
